title: caloncoba flagelliflora
intro: Caloncoba flagelliflora, also known as the stick tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihelmintic activities, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Caloncoba flagelliflora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive problems, as well as its ability to act as an anthelmintic, helping to expel intestinal parasites. Caloncoba flagelliflora can be prepared in various forms, including a decoction made by boiling the bark or leaves in water, or as a powder or tincture that can be taken orally to utilize its medicinal properties.
